body: the `for` loop does not compile on the GPU. This PR follows the same implementation we have for closure tuples in `TurbulenceClosures`. I have also added a test
title: Correct diffusive dissipation for tuple of closures

body:

julia> using Oceananigans

julia> u = FieldTimeSeries("one_degree_surface_fields.jld2", "u"; backend = OnDisk())

├── grid: 360×180×40 ImmersedBoundaryGrid{Float64, Periodic, Oceananigans.Grids.RightConnected, Bounded} on CPU with 5×5×4 halo
├── indices: (:, :, 40:40)
├── time_indexing: Linear()
├── backend: OnDisk
├── path: one_degree_surface_fields.jld2
└── name: u

julia> u[1].boundary_conditions
missing

title: Don't search for missing bcs

body: E.g., `Models` may need to depend on `Simulations`.
title: Shuffle import order to clarify dependencies

body:
At the moment, a nonlinear free surface conserves global tracer (except for on a tripolar grid which I ll address after this PR), but does not preserve the uniformity of a tracer.

This PR makes sure that an initially uniform tracer remains uniform by stepping the grid with the same timestepper used for tracers (density). 
I have also added a tracer constancy preservation test in the test suite.

After this problem is solved, we have to deal with the issue of reconciliation of the grid with the free surface evolved in the barotropic step which at the moment are different: i.e. the grid is evolved with the large baroclinic step to ensure consistency with the tracer, while the free surface is evolved either implicitly or split explicitly but with another method that will make the two formulations diverge slightly.

This PR also changes the stored tendency, storing $\sigma * G$ for tracers instead of storing just $G$

labels.description: So things don't blow up and boil the lobsters alive
labels.name: numerics 🧮

title: Ensure constancy preservation with nonlinear free surface
